name: research-grants
tagline: Guidance skill for writing NSF, NIH, DOE, DARPA and Taiwan NSTC grant proposals, with agency-specific structure, budgets and review criteria
verdict: Clean, well-scoped writing-guidance skill with no exfiltration, obfuscation, or manipulation attempts. The one network path (optional AI figure generation via OpenRouter) is honestly disclosed and opt-in, requiring the user's own API key. Main issues are hygiene: SKILL.md is ~929 lines, nearly double the ~500-line budget, and it lacks explicit 'when not to u

highlights.summary: Optional figure generation via the sibling 'scientific-schematics' skill sends the user's prompt to OpenRouter (line 54: 'AI schematic generation sends your prompt to OpenRouter (a third-party API)').
No 'when NOT to use this skill' section exists anywhere in the file, so a user can't quickly tell when this skill is the wrong tool (e.g., non-federal/foundation grants, non-US/Taiwan agencies).

Despite declaring allowed-tools Read Write Edit Bash (line 4), the skill never states what file(s) it actually writes, where, or in what format — no explicit output contract for the drafting work it e
